summary: Verified Navbar bug fix — SAS Energy Solutions wordmark + tagline now render next to the logo image across desktop (1920, 1280) and tablet (768) viewports; correctly hidden on <640px mobile. Color transitions properly on scroll. No layout regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.js correctly implements the fix: + sibling containing 'SAS Energy Solutions' bold text and 'Think Future · Get Benefits' tagline. Color classes correctly switch based on `scrolled` state (text-white -> text-slate-900; text-[#7CE39E] -> text-[#16A34A]).

Minor console warning (non-blocking): 'Please ensure that the container has a non-static position, like relative/fixed/absolute...' — likely from framer-motion useScroll on some section container. Not related to this fix but worth investigating.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Navbar wordmark fix verified. data-testid='nav-logo' contains both and the wordmark span. Visibility confirmed via bounding box (184x35 px at 1920). Text color on transparent hero = rgb(255,255,255); after scroll >100px = rgb(15,23,42). Mobile toggle (data-testid='nav-mobile-toggle') opens drawer with nav-mobile-{home|about|...} entries.
rca of the issue: Original bug: company name text missing from header. Root cause: only logo image was rendered, no sibling wordmark span. Fix (already applied by main agent): added <span class='leading-tight hidden sm:block'> wrapping two child spans — 'SAS Energy Solutions' (bold) and 'Think Future · Get Benefits' (uppercase tagline) — with color classes conditional on scroll state. Verified present in DOM and visible from sm breakpoint (>=640px) upward.
